name: cf_bp_main_instances

safeReportingNotes: Use for read-only process instance status and lifecycle reporting.
Use with cf_bp_worker_instances when reporting on suspended, interrupted, or terminated task state.
Use process instance records for read-only Forms metadata reporting.
The status column stores the main Forms process instance lifecycle state. Use worker instance state as well when reporting suspended or task-level conditions.
warnings: Do not update runtime instance records directly.
Forms instance monitoring status is calculated with worker instance state; do not interpret this column by itself for suspended-task reporting.
RunningWithSuspendedTask = 6 is a Forms application state label and does not actually exist as a stored value in cf_bp_main_instances.status.

safeReportingNotes: Use stored file payload records for read-only reporting after reviewing whether values include user-entered or sensitive content.

columns.name: unique_id
columns.purpose: Unique identifier associated with the stored file payload record.

columns.name: file_name
columns.purpose: File Name value for the stored file payload record.

columns.name: file_type
columns.purpose: Type or category value for file type on the stored file payload record.

columns.name: data
columns.purpose: Stored data payload for the record; review contents before reporting.

columns.name: created_by_snapshot_id
columns.purpose: User snapshot identifier for the creator.

columns.name: date_created
columns.purpose: Timestamp when the record was created.

reportingExamples.title: Inspect representative rows
reportingExamples.note: Use TOP with a narrow column list when orienting yourself to table shape. Do not update rows.

reportingExamples.title: Recent records by created_by_snapshot_id
reportingExamples.note: Confirm the date column meaning before using it for operational reporting.
reportingExamples.sql:
SELECT TOP (100)
  [file_name],
  [file_type],
  [date_created],
  [unique_id],
  [data],
  [created_by_snapshot_id]
FROM [dbo].[file_table]
WHERE [created_by_snapshot_id] IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY [created_by_snapshot_id] DESC;

reportingExamples.title: Counts by file_type
reportingExamples.note: Status and type values are product-specific. Validate the values before publishing metrics.
reportingExamples.sql:
SELECT
  [file_type],
  COUNT_BIG(*) AS row_count
FROM [dbo].[file_table]
GROUP BY [file_type]
ORDER BY row_count DESC;
